children.text: Car Dashboard
Welcome to Car Dashboard!
Please read the following frequently asked questions before getting started.
What features does the pro version have?

Currently the pro version will remove advertising from the application, will show you your current street location, and will support further development of the application. You can find the pro version in the Android Market or Amazon Appstore.
Can I help translate the app into another language?
Absolutely. Is the app not available in your language? Is there something mistranslated? Go to our Crowdin project and help us make the app better for everyone.
http://crowdin.net/project/car-dashboard/invite
I have a problem. How can I get help?
Send us an email at support@mandaria.net or use the 'Email Feedback' button in the menu. We will get back to you as soon as possible. We cannot help you if you leave a comment on the Android Market.
support@mandaria.net
Where can I find the latest news about the application?
Follow us on Twitter for information about future updates.
http://twitter.com/mandariasoft
Which weather service do you use?
Our weather services are provided by the free Open Weather Map service.
http://openweathermap.org
Can I use this app without GPS?

The core functionality of this application (speedometer, speed alerts, location, weather, altitude) requires GPS in order to work. Only the clock, compass, and custom launcher buttons will work without GPS.
How does the compass work?

When traveling faster than 2 mph (~3km/h), the application will use your velocity (speed + direction) from the GPS to determine your heading. Otherwise, the phone's accelerometer and magnetometer sensors are used to calculate your direction.
Why is the orientation locked?

The orientation is locked to prevent the phone from changing orientations while driving due to rough terrain/roads. You can change the orientation that is locked or disabled it altogether in the settings.
How accurate is the speedometer?

The speedometer is as accurate as your phone's GPS receiver. Therefore a G1's GPS receiver will not perform as well as a more recent Android device. Additionally, your GPS accuracy can be affected by satellite positions, noise in the radio signal, atmospheric conditions, and large barriers (such as buildings or mountains) to the signal. These conditions can produce GPS accuracy errors up to 30 meters. In order to alleviate these accuracy errors, the application will by default ignore speeds over 150 mph to prevent erroneous speed spikes (this can be disabled in the settings). The application will also ignore GPS data if the accuracy reported by the GPS is not at least 200 meters accurate (this value can be adjusted in the settings).
What do the GPS statuses mean?

When your phone is 'Searching for GPS' then a GPS signal has not yet been acquired. When it finds a GPS lock, then status will change to 'GPS Found' and then 'GPS Locked' meaning that the application is ready to use GPS features (speedometer, weather, location, etc.). If the GPS is experiencing low accuracy issues, then the status will change to 'GPS Low Accuracy'. Beside of the GPS status description is a count of the number of satellites that are currently being used to determine your signal.
How do the speed alerts work?

A speed alert will trigger when your current speed has exceeded the alert's speed limit. The alert can change the speedometer's color, the screen's border color and/or play a sound when activated. When your current speed drops to the alert's speed limit, then that specific alert will no longer be active. The current active speed alert will always be the highest speed alert below your current speed.
How do I enable sounds for speed alerts?

By default, none of the speed alerts have sounds enabled. Go to 'Manage Speed Alerts' and tap on the speed alert that you wish to enable sounds on. On the next screen, scroll down and enable sound alerts and choose a sound to play.
Why isn't the location/weather updating?

These features require a GPS signal in order to work. Additionally, these features rely on 3rd party services and those services may sometimes not return data when requested. The application will keep retrying to update the information until it succeeds.
How do I switch to US customary/metric units?

You can change the default speed alerts to mph or km/h by going to 'Manage Speed Alerts', pressing your menu button, and tapping 'Restore Default Alerts'. To change your speed, altitude, or temperature units, go to 'Settings'
How do the custom launcher buttons work?

In order to use the custom launcher buttons, you must first set the shortcut (application) that the button will launch when pressed. You can change the shortcut, edit the description of the shortcut, or delete the shortcut by long pressing on the shortcut's button. Use these custom launcher buttons to get quick access to your important apps, such as your music player, podcatcher, navigation, etc.
How many custom launcher buttons can I have?
You can have 20 custom launcher buttons. Swipe left or right to access more of your custom launcher buttons.
Why does it take so long to select a shortcut?
The more applications you have installed on your device, the longer it will take to show a list of available shortcuts and applications to add. Please be patient, the list will eventually show up.
Why won't my shortcut add to the custom launcher button?

If you experience problems attaching a shortcut to a custom launcher button, then it is most likely your phone's memory is full and is causing garbage collection. Please try rebooting your phone and try to add your shortcuts again. Additionally, you can free up memory by uninstalling unused applications or removing running background services.
How can I add a shortcut to directly dial or message a contact?
Some phones (typically Motorola phones) do not have a shortcut to directly dial or message a contact. You can download Any Cut in the Android Market in order to fix this problem.
My contact shortcut isn't showing the correct picture?

When adding a shortcut to a contact, it uses the picture from your Google contact. Facebook sync does not replace your Google contact's picture, so you must either manually set a Google contact picture for that person or use an application such as SyncMyPix to sync your Facebook pictures to your Google contacts.
Start using the app
